name: Zeus's Sunday Chicken Dinner
recipeInstructions:
1. Thaw filo dough according to manufacturers instructions. In some cases, this may be overnight.
2. Preheat oven to 350°F.
3. Brush the bottom and sides of a deep 10-inch pie dish with some olive oil.
4. Rinse spinach well and place in a proofing bowl. Microwave on High for 1 to 2 minutes, stirring once, until wilted and cooked through. Alternatively, cook spinach in a dry skillet until wilted. When cool enough to handle, squeeze the moisture from the spinach.
5. Chop the spinach into fine pieces and place in a medium mixing bowl.
6. Separate one egg into two small bowls. Break the remaining egg into the bowl with the egg white and beat lightly. Set both bowls aside.
7. Dice the chicken breasts and add them to the bowl with the spinach. Pour in ouzo if using.
8. Add celery, green onions, mint, basil, marjoram, tarragon, pepper, feta, and kasseri cheese.
9. Pour in the beaten egg mixture and mix thoroughly.
10. Place a sheet of filo on a clean counter and keep covered with plastic wrap or a damp towel. Brush the sheet with olive oil before placing it in the pie dish.
11. Start by placing half of one sheet in the dish and the other half extending beyond the edge.
12. Place the second sheet on top, this time extending over the edge in the opposite direction.
13. Place two more sheets at right angles to the first two, forming a cross.
14. Repeat the process with the remaining four sheets.
15. Spread the chicken mixture evenly into the pan.
16. Fold the overhanging layers of filo up and across the chicken, sheet by sheet, brushing with olive oil after each sheet is in place.
17. Finish smoothing out the top with a final brushing of olive oil.
18. Beat the egg yolk lightly and brush the top with the beaten yolk.
19. Pierce the top several times with a fork to allow steam to escape.
20. Bake for 50 minutes, until the crust is crisp and golden. Let cool on a rack for 10 minutes before cutting.
21. Serve hot or at room temperature.
